mappings.rationale: Directly covers theories of learning, conditioning, and the psychology of learning including transfer of training (A, B).
Directly covers memory, retention, forgetting, and memory manipulation, including abnormalities of memory (E, F).
Directly covers the higher thought processes including reasoning, problem solving, and deduction (G.1).
Covers the psychology of higher thought processes, consciousness, and reasoning (G.1).
Directly relevant to perceptual learning -- how perceptual skills develop through experience (C).
Covers the neural basis of learning, memory formation, and higher cognitive processes (A, E, G).
Covers brain mechanisms underlying learning, memory storage, and the neural substrates of thought.
Covers active learning, cognitive development, inner speech, and Piaget's work on concept formation in children (D, G.2).
Pavlov's conditioning research is foundational to modern learning theories (A.1).
Covers language, meaning, and speech, directly relevant to the role of language in higher thought processes (G.2, G.3).
Covers how language structures and phonology influence thought, relevant to language in higher thought processes (G.2).
Directly covers the intersection of language and cognition, relevant to the role of language in thought and meaning (G.2, G.3).

mappings.vsiAuthor: Emma Borg and Sarah A. Fisher
mappings.rationale: Directly covers meaning, reference, truth, and word meaning -- the core of section G.3.
Relevant to the role of conscious awareness in learning and concept formation.
Covers cognitive psychology, developmental psychology, and the broad framework for understanding learning and thinking.
Covers the applied dimension of learning theories including teaching methods and transfer of training (A.3).
Covers memory loss, diagnosis, and cognitive decline, directly relevant to abnormalities of memory including amnesia (F).
Covers a specific learning difficulty affecting reading and perceptual processing, relevant to perceptual and conceptual learning (C, D).
Covers creative thinking and invention, relevant to the higher thought processes and novel concept formation (G.1).
Covers the cognitive processes of imagination and ideation, relevant to higher thought processes (G.1).
Covers interpretation, meaning, and the role of language in understanding -- relevant to meaning and language in thought (G.2, G.3).
Wittgenstein's philosophy of language addresses meaning and the relationship between language and thought (G.2, G.3).
Machine learning provides comparative models for understanding human learning theories and concept formation.
